questions: Should the drag handle be always visible, or follow the current hover-to-reveal pattern used by the arrow buttons? (Drag handles are typically always visible for discoverability, but this changes the at-rest appearance of the track label.)

Is there any keyboard accessibility requirement for track reordering? The arrows provided keyboard-accessible reorder. With drag-only, we'd lose that unless we add keyboard sortable support via @dnd-kit's KeyboardSensor.
Should we support dragging a track from one kind to another (e.g., promoting an audio track to visual)? Currently the system treats kind boundaries as hard — I'm assuming we keep that constraint.
success_criteria: Up/down buttons (or drag) correctly disabled/constrained for tracks at the boundary of their kind group
Audio tracks with 2+ tracks can be reordered (currently broken)
Reorder across kind boundaries is prevented — visual tracks stay above audio tracks
Drag handle replaces arrow buttons in TrackLabel hover state
Dragging a track visually reorders it within its kind group and persists the change
No interference between track drag and existing clip drag or external drop functionality
Track order survives save/load cycle (sortTracksByKind preserves relative order within kind)
assumptions: The visual-before-audio track ordering invariant (enforced by sortTracksByKind) should be maintained — no cross-kind reordering
The existing app-level DndContext should NOT be reused for track drags — a nested DndContext is safer to avoid interference with clip/shot DnD
The GripVertical drag handle will live in the hover overlay (same position as current arrow buttons) to keep the at-rest UI clean, unless user says otherwise
We'll keep @dnd-kit/sortable (already at v10) — no new dependencies needed
The handleReorderTrack function can be replaced entirely by the drag-based handleMoveTrack — we don't need to keep the directional API
